USE_MODSECURITY_CRS_PLUGINS.help: Enable OWASP Core Rule Set plugins.
USE_MODSECURITY_CRS_PLUGINS.label: Use Core Rule Set Plugins

MODSECURITY_CRS_VERSION.help: Version of the OWASP Core Rule Set to use with ModSecurity (3 or 4). Note: nightly is deprecated and will default to v4.
MODSECURITY_CRS_VERSION.label: Core Rule Set Version

MODSECURITY_CRS_PLUGINS.help: List of OWASP CRS plugins (plugin-name[/tag] or URL) to download and install (separated with spaces). (Not compatible with CRS version 3)
MODSECURITY_CRS_PLUGINS.label: Core Rule Set Plugins

USE_MODSECURITY_GLOBAL_CRS.help: Use ModSecurity CRS in global mode to improve rules loading when you have many services.
USE_MODSECURITY_GLOBAL_CRS.label: ModSecurity global CRS mode

MODSECURITY_SEC_AUDIT_LOG_PARTS.help: SecAuditLogParts directive of ModSecurity. Parts A and Z are always included automatically as prefix and suffix.
MODSECURITY_SEC_AUDIT_LOG_PARTS.label: SecAuditLogParts

multiselect.label: I - Compact request body (alternative to C)

MODSECURITY_SEC_REQUEST_BODY_LIMIT.help: SecRequestBodyLimit directive of ModSecurity. Maximum request body size that ModSecurity will inspect (supports k/m/g suffixes, e.g. 10m). Requests exceeding this limit are handled according to SecRequestBodyLimitAction (Reject by default). When empty, derived from MAX_CLIENT_SIZE. Set 0 for no limit (not recommended — may cause out-of-memory kills). NGINX enforces MAX_CLIENT_SIZE before ModSecurity, so setting this higher than MAX_CLIENT_SIZE has no effect.
MODSECURITY_SEC_REQUEST_BODY_LIMIT.label: SecRequestBodyLimit

MODSECURITY_SEC_REQUEST_BODY_LIMIT_ACTIO.help: SecRequestBodyLimitAction directive of ModSecurity. Reject returns HTTP 413 for requests exceeding the body limit. ProcessPartial inspects only the portion within the limit and forwards the full request to the backend (useful for large file uploads, but reduces WAF coverage for non-file content types like JSON or form data).
MODSECURITY_SEC_REQUEST_BODY_LIMIT_ACTIO.label: SecRequestBodyLimitAction
